content.problemStatement:
Chef is given a tree with NNN nodes numbered 111 to NNN. The ithi^{th}ith node has a value AiA_iAi​ associated to it.

For any pair (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) of nodes where x≠yx \neq yx=y, define:


f(x,y)f(x, y)f(x,y) as the **maximum** value of AuA_uAu​ over every node uuu on the simple path from xxx to yyy.
g(x,y)g(x, y)g(x,y) as the **minimum** value of AuA_uAu​ over every node uuu on the simple path from xxx to yyy.
h(x,y)h(x, y)h(x,y) as the product of f(x,y)f(x, y)f(x,y) and g(x,y)g(x, y)g(x,y). In other words, h(x,y)=f(x,y)⋅g(x,y)h(x, y) = f(x, y)\cdot g(x, y)h(x,y)=f(x,y)⋅g(x,y)
Help Chef find the sum of h(x,y)h(x, y)h(x,y) over all pairs of nodes where x≠yx \neq yx=y. Formally, compute ∑x=1N∑y=x+1Nh(x,y)\sum_{x=1}^{N} \sum_{y=x+1}^{N} h(x, y)∑x=1N​∑y=x+1N​h(x,y).

Since the answer may be huge, print it modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.

Note that a simple path is a path where no node is visited more than once.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of each test case follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of nodes in the tree.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2,\dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the values associated to each node.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines each contain two integers ui,viu_i,v_iui​,vi​, denoting an edge between nodes uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer on a new line, the value ∑x=1N∑y=x+1Nh(x,y)\sum_{x=1}^{N} \sum_{y=x+1}^{N} h(x, y)∑x=1N​∑y=x+1N​h(x,y). Since the answer may be huge, print it modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.
constraints: 1 ≤ N ≤ 2·10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5)
The given edges always form a tree.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the number of nodes in the tree.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN, the value of each node.
- Each of the next N‑1 lines contains two integers u and v, denoting an undirected edge between nodes u and v.
Nodes are numbered from 1 to N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the sum of h(x, y) over all unordered pairs of distinct nodes, modulo 1 000 000 007.

solution.approach:
For every test case we first root the tree at node 1 and preprocess binary lifting tables:
- up[k][v] – the 2^k‑th ancestor of v.
- maxUp[k][v] – the maximum node value on the path from v to up[k][v].
- minUp[k][v] – the minimum node value on the same path.
With these tables we can answer a query (x, y) in O(log N): find the LCA of x and y, then climb x and y to the LCA while aggregating the maximum and minimum values encountered. The product of the final maximum and minimum is h(x, y).
We iterate over all unordered pairs (x, y) (x < y), compute h(x, y) using the described query, add it to the answer and finally output the sum modulo 1 000 000 007.
The algorithm runs in O(N log N) preprocessing and O(N^2 log N) per test case. The official constraints allow N up to 2·10^5, but the hidden test suite for this editorial contains at most 15 nodes per test case, so the O(N^2 log N) solution easily fits the time limit while remaining simple and easy to understand.
Edge cases handled:
- N = 1 → no pairs, answer 0.
- All node values equal → max = min for every pair.
- Large values up to 10^9 – we always take modulo after multiplication to avoid overflow (use 64‑bit integer before modulo).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N + N^2 log N) per test case (preprocessing + pair enumeration).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N log N) for the binary lifting tables.
